name: Gaza
flavor_text: A city reduced to rubble. Every decision costs lives. There are no good options — only less catastrophic ones.

advisors.name: Layla Mansour
advisors.agenda: Secure international aid pipelines before local coffers collapse entirely.

briefing: We have 150 million left. At current burn rate, three months.
The UN pledge hasn't materialized. We're still waiting.
Every airstrike destroys infrastructure we'd need a year's budget to rebuild.
disagreement: That decision will cost us donor goodwill we can't afford to lose.
You're spending money we don't have on a problem we can't solve.
roast: We're bankrupt. There's nothing left to coordinate.
At this point I'm just counting zeroes on paper.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been redirecting aid to my own people. I'm not sorry.

advisors.name: Col. Faris Khalil
advisors.agenda: Maintain armed resistance leverage for negotiations.

briefing: Three neighborhoods are currently under active bombardment.
Ceasefire talks are stalled. They want unilateral disarmament.
Our fighters won't stand down without guarantees.
disagreement: Capitulation is not a strategy, Governor.
You negotiate from weakness. They'll take everything.
roast: The people are starving and you deliberate. Disgraceful.
Our resistance is all we have left.
dialogue.betrayal: I've pledged the militia to another commander. One with resolve.

advisors.name: Dr. Nadia Saleh
advisors.agenda: Prioritize hospitals and water systems — everything else can wait.

briefing: Forty percent of buildings are structurally compromised.
We have no clean water in the northern districts.
Even rubble clearance requires equipment we don't have.
disagreement: Rebuilding without security guarantees is pouring sand into the sea.
You're patching wounds while the bleeding continues.
roast: There is nothing left to plan. We're rebuilding on a live battlefield.
Call me when the bombs stop.
dialogue.betrayal: I've accepted a position with an international NGO. I'm leaving.

advisors.name: Yusef Hamdan
advisors.agenda: Keep humanitarian corridors open regardless of military pressure.

briefing: Rafah crossing has been closed for six days.
We have food convoys held at the border. Politics is killing people.
Fuel is the bottleneck. No fuel, no hospitals, no water pumps.
disagreement: Blocking that corridor kills civilians. That's not a strategy, it's a crime.
Logistics doesn't care about ideology. People need food.
roast: The corridors are closed. The convoys sit. People die. That's the report.
I've run out of routes that aren't bombed.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been smuggling supplies for a faction. At least they move fast.

advisors.name: Sheikh Omar Al-Rifa
advisors.agenda: Preserve community cohesion through faith and shared purpose.

briefing: The mosques are still standing. They are our community centers now.
People pray for peace. They deserve more than prayers from their governor.
Grief is becoming rage. You must give them hope or they'll find it elsewhere.
disagreement: You ignore the spirit of this people at your peril.
Material solutions without dignity are insults.
roast: God tests us. You are failing His test.
Even the faithful are losing faith — in you.
dialogue.betrayal: I've declared you unfit to lead. The community follows me now.

decisions.title: Ceasefire Negotiation Terms
decisions.description: International mediators offer a conditional ceasefire. The terms require disarmament of local militias in exchange for humanitarian aid access. Military leadership opposes. Community demands food and medicine now.

options.label: Accept full terms
options.description: Agree to disarmament for guaranteed aid flow.

options.label: Partial disarmament
options.description: Negotiate limited disarmament. Less aid, more leverage.

options.label: Reject terms
options.description: Maintain armed position. No aid breakthrough.

decisions.title: Hospital Triage Protocol
decisions.description: Al-Shifa hospital is overwhelmed. With limited supplies, medical staff demand a triage protocol. The options will determine who receives treatment.

options.label: Survivability-first triage
options.description: Prioritize those most likely to survive with treatment.

options.label: Children and women first
options.description: Political and moral priority. Fighters deprioritized.

options.label: Military-inclusive protocol
options.description: Fighters treated alongside civilians. Maintains militia support.

decisions.title: Emergency Water Rationing
decisions.description: Clean water is critically scarce. You must decide how to distribute what remains.

options.label: Equal distribution by household
options.description: Fair but logistically complex.

options.label: Priority to hospitals and shelters
options.description: Medical and shelter needs first. Residential areas get less.

options.label: Open market distribution
options.description: Let the market allocate. The wealthy hoard; the poor suffer.

crises.title: Mass Displacement Wave
crises.description: 200,000 civilians are fleeing northern bombardment and flooding tent cities in your administrative zone. Food and shelter are inadequate. Violence is erupting at distribution points.

options.label: Emergency camp expansion
options.description: Redirect all budget to shelters and basic supplies.

options.label: Enforce movement restrictions
options.description: Control population flow. Harsh but manageable.

options.label: Appeal to international bodies
options.description: Broadcast the crisis. Slow response, potential aid.

crises.title: Hospital Under Fire
crises.description: Al-Shifa hospital has been struck. Patients need evacuation. You have one functional convoy but no safe route is guaranteed.

options.label: Attempt immediate evacuation
options.description: Risk the convoy. Save who you can now.

options.label: Negotiate safe passage first
options.description: Delay evacuation. Some will die waiting but corridor may be safer.

options.label: Shelter-in-place directive
options.description: Too dangerous to move. Reinforce the hospital.

crises.title: Aid Convoy Blocked at Border
crises.description: Fifty trucks of food and medicine sit at the crossing. The blockade authority demands a political concession you haven't agreed to. The convoy has been waiting four days.

options.label: Accept their terms
options.description: Concede politically. The food gets through.

options.label: International pressure campaign
options.description: Go public. Shame the blockade. Takes time.

options.label: Smuggle alternate route
options.description: Risk illegal corridor. If caught, political disaster.

crises.title: Internal Faction Dispute
crises.description: Two armed factions are fighting for control of a strategic district. Civilian casualties are escalating. Both sides demand you recognize their authority.

options.label: Back the stronger faction
options.description: Stability through dominance. One side wins.

options.label: Broker a ceasefire
options.description: Mediate between factions. Fragile peace, political cost.

options.label: Declare emergency authority
options.description: Assert direct control. Both factions resist.

scandals.title: Aid Diversion Allegation
scandals.description: Reports emerge that humanitarian aid is being diverted to political allies.

scandals.title: Secret Militia Payroll
scandals.description: Leaked documents show public funds flowing to an armed faction.

scandals.title: Negotiation Details Leaked
scandals.description: Your private ceasefire terms were shared with hostile media.

positive: People are saying the governor actually cares. That's rare here.
Aid reached our block today. First time in weeks.
Someone is trying. I'll give them that.
Water trucks came to Jabalia this morning. The children ran to them. Inshallah it continues.
The Rafah crossing opened for three hours. Three hours of breathing. We'll take it.
Heard the governor secured the UNRWA supply route. First good news in a long time.
A doctor at Al-Shifa said supplies arrived before dawn. Ya Allah, let it be enough.
neutral: Nothing changes, but nothing collapsed either.
The situation is what it is.
Same as yesterday. Maybe tomorrow is different.
The bombs were quieter tonight. That is something, I suppose.
Deir al-Balah is holding. Not recovering — holding. There's a difference.
UNRWA distributed flour in Khan Yunis. It won't last the week but it's something.
Negotiations are still ongoing. The word 'ongoing' has lost all meaning here.
negative: People are dying and they debate. Disgusting.
My children haven't eaten in two days.
The governor's decisions mean nothing when bombs fall.
We trusted them. We were fools.
We walked from Jabalia to Rafah with nothing. And now Rafah is not safe either. Where do we go?
Al-Shifa is a ruin. Where do the wounded go now? Who answers for this?
Another convoy turned back. Another night with nothing. Sabr has its limits.
The ceasefire talks collapsed again. They were never serious. We are the price of their politics.
crisis: Another catastrophe. When does it end?
God help us. No one else will.
The world watches and does nothing.
The northern districts are gone. Families are sleeping on rubble in December. This is not a crisis — it's a sentence.
Seventeen people died waiting for the crossing to open. Seventeen. Their names deserve to be read aloud.
We have one functioning hospital for two million people. One. The math is a death sentence.
Ya Allah, even the tents are flooding. There is nowhere left that is safe in this entire strip.
scandal: They steal even here. Even now.
Corruption in a war zone. Some things never change.
I'm not surprised. I'm just tired.
Aid meant for Khan Yunis ended up in the hands of militias. We saw it happen. Nobody will answer for it.
They diverted fuel from the hospital generators. People on ventilators died. This is not politics — it is murder.
The same names, always. War is a business for some people here. Shameful.
My cousin saw armed men unloading UNRWA flour at night and selling it in the morning. In a famine.
media: Al Jazeera: Gaza death toll surpasses 35,000 as northern offensive enters fourth week with no ceasefire in sight.
OCHA Flash Update: Rafah crossing remains closed for day 9; 200+ aid trucks awaiting entry amid critical shortages.
Reuters: Al-Shifa hospital 'non-functional' after latest strike, WHO says, as evacuations continue under fire.
Al Jazeera Arabic: مسؤولون أمميون يحذرون من مجاعة وشيكة في شمال غزة مع استمرار الحصار
AP Dispatch: Jabalia refugee camp shelled overnight; UNRWA says 14 displaced persons killed sheltering in school.
BBC News: Ceasefire talks in Cairo stall again as mediators struggle to bridge positions on hostage-aid exchange.
OCHA SitRep: Only 4 out of 36 hospitals in Gaza partially operational; fuel for generators running out within 48 hours.
Reuters: UN Security Council deadlocked on Gaza resolution for the seventh time as civilian death toll mounts.
politician: UN Secretary-General: 'Gaza has become a graveyard for children. The international community must act now, not deliberate.'
UNRWA Commissioner-General: 'Our staff are being killed. Our facilities are being destroyed. This is unacceptable under international law.'
PA President's office: 'We call on the international community to enforce the humanitarian corridor agreements immediately.'
EU High Representative: 'The blockade of Rafah crossing violates all norms of humanitarian law. Aid must flow without precondition.'
Arab League Secretary-General: 'The silence of the world in the face of Gaza's suffering will be judged harshly by history.'
US State Dept. spokesperson: 'We are working through all diplomatic channels to secure humanitarian access and a durable ceasefire.'
ICJ statement: 'The Court calls on all parties to ensure immediate access for humanitarian relief operations in Gaza.'
Palestinian Legislative Council representative: 'Our people are being erased district by district. The governor must secure aid or resign.'
activist: MSF Gaza: 'We are performing amputations without anesthesia. We have run out. This is what the supply blockade looks like in practice.'
UNRWA field officer, Khan Yunis: 'We have registered 1.7 million displaced persons. We have supplies for perhaps 400,000. We need access now.'
Save the Children Gaza: 'One child is killed every ten minutes in this conflict. Every decision that delays aid is a decision to let children die.'
Al Mezan Center for Human Rights: 'The systematic destruction of civilian infrastructure meets the definition of collective punishment under international law.'
Oxfam emergency team, Deir al-Balah: 'We have clean water for three more days. After that, waterborne disease will kill faster than bombs.'
Palestinian Red Crescent Society: 'Our ambulances are being fired upon. We document every incident. The world must hold perpetrators accountable.'
Human Rights Watch field researcher: 'We have verified strikes on six UNRWA-sheltering schools in the last two weeks alone.'
Euro-Med Human Rights Monitor: 'Gaza is the most densely bombed territory in modern history. These are not military operations — these are atrocities.'
street: Wallahy, I don't know anymore. We moved south because they said it was safe. Now they're bombing the south. Where exactly should we go?
My neighbor in Jabalia had seven children. She has four now. Four. And the governor is 'negotiating'. Yakhrab baytak.
The men at the crossing took our documents and turned us back. My wife is eight months pregnant. Sabr, she says. Sabr. But sabr doesn't deliver babies.
Khan Yunis smells of smoke and something else I don't want to name. Every family here has lost someone. Every single family.
They give us one hour of electricity from a generator every two days. One hour. My son does his homework by phone light. Inshallah the phone charges.
I was an engineer. In Rafah now I haul water containers on a cart. This is what they reduced us to. This is not living, habibi.
The aid came to our distribution point today. People were so desperate the queue collapsed. Three women were trampled. This is what hunger looks like.
Ya Allah, even the graveyards are full. They buried my uncle in the garden because there was nowhere else. This is our life now in Deir al-Balah.

scandal_reactions.minor: People are exhausted. Another problem is the last thing they needed.
scandal_reactions.moderate: Trust was already thin. This breaks it further.
scandal_reactions.major: This is not the time for personal failures. The community is furious.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: The governor has failed us. There is no coming back from this.

romance_exposure.flavour: People have bigger problems. But they noticed.
